name: Jimmy McGriff
description: American hard bop and soul-jazz organist and organ trio bandleader (1936-2008)
abstract: James Harrell McGriff  (April 3, 1936 – May 24, 2008) was an American hard bop and soul-jazz organist and organ trio bandleader.

concerts.label: Blue Note Records and WBGO Benefit Jazz Concert
JVC Jazz Festival: Aretha Franklin, Singer

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Easley
about_quotes.evidence: I probably played with more great organ player than anybody on the planet. Jimmy Smith, Jimmy McGriff, you can’t name but maybe two organ players that I didn’t play with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Easley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 1, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: George Benson
about_quotes.evidence: Jimmy Smith, Jack McDuff, Johnny “Hammond” Smith, and quite a few, Jimmy McGriff and Groove Holmes. These were all still our favorite guys.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Benson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Doug Carn
about_quotes.evidence: My uncle was good friends with Shirley and Stan and you know Trudy Pitts, Jimmy McGriff, I followed every one of them.
about_quotes.doc_title: Doug Carn interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 24, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Lou Donaldson
about_quotes.evidence: Then McDuff and Groove Holmes and Jimmy McGriff and all of them came in later. We started in Rochester... We had a little circuit that we played... Jimmy McGriff. A lot of organ players going around.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lou Donaldson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
